content: The multi-stage Mars colonization mission will consist of four interdependent phases: habitat construction, life support systems, food production, and communication infrastructure. Each phase will depend on the successful completion of the previous one, requiring a structured approach to planning and execution. A detailed breakdown of subtasks and agent assignments is essential for effective collaboration.

The Mars colonization mission will be broken down into specific subtasks for each phase: for habitat construction, we need site selection, design, and materials procurement; for life support systems, we need air filtration, water recycling, and waste management; for food production, we need crop selection, greenhouse construction, and irrigation systems; and for communication infrastructure, we need satellite deployment and ground station establishment. Each subtask will be assigned to agents based on their expertise to ensure efficiency and effectiveness. The completion of each phase will depend on the successful execution of its preceding phase.
